epigraph.text: For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ shall all be made alive.

content.text:
Two triangles are congruent if they have exactly the same shape and size. 
This means all corresponding parts—sides and angles—are congruent.

content.title: Congruent Triangles
content.content:
Two triangles are **congruent** if and 
only if their corresponding sides and corresponding angles are congruent.

content.text:
When we write $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle DEF$, the order of vertices 
indicates the correspondence:

items:
$\angle A \cong \angle D$, $\angle B \cong \angle E$, 
 $\angle C \cong \angle F$

content.title: Properties of Congruence
content.content:
Triangle congruence is:

- **Reflexive:** $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle ABC$

- **Symmetric:** If $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle DEF$, 
 then $\triangle DEF \cong \triangle ABC$

- **Transitive:** If $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle DEF$ 
 and $\triangle DEF \cong \triangle GHI$, then 
 $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle GHI$
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Third Angle Theorem
content.content:
If two angles of one triangle are congruent to two angles of another 
triangle, then the third angles are also congruent.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Let $\angle A \cong \angle D$ and $\angle B \cong \angle E$ in triangles 
$ABC$ and $DEF$.

By the Triangle Sum Theorem:
\[
 m\angle A + m\angle B + m\angle C = 180°
\]
\[
 m\angle D + m\angle E + m\angle F = 180°
\]

Since $m\angle A = m\angle D$ and $m\angle B = m\angle E$:
\[
 m\angle C = 180° - m\angle A - m\angle B = 180° - m\angle D - m\angle E = m\angle F
\]

Therefore, $\angle C \cong \angle F$.

marginNotes.text:
Describe the temple to the house of 
Israel... that they may be ashamed of their iniquities; and let them 
measure the pattern.
